title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
logline: The story of the 1969 trial of seven defendants charged by the federal government with conspiracy and inciting to riot at the 1968 Democratic National Convention in Chicago.
protagonist: Tom Hayden and the Chicago 7 defendants
antagonistOrConflict: Judge Julius Hoffman and the federal government's prosecution
tone: Dramatic with satirical elements, politically charged

actBreaks.description: The trial begins in earnest as all defendants are introduced in the courtroom and the legal battle commences.

validation.ruling: Scene #41 marks the end of Act 1 by transitioning from setup to the actual trial proceedings, with David Stahl taking the stand on Trial Day 3. This represents the fundamental shift from pre-trial preparation and character introduction to the main courtroom drama that will dominate the rest of the story.

actBreaks.description: Bobby Seale is bound and gagged in court, representing the trial's descent into chaos and the system's authoritarian response.

validation.ruling: Scene #156 shows Kunstler questioning Tom about his inflammatory statement 'If blood is going to flow, let it flow all over the city,' representing the climactic moment of Tom's testimony and the peak of courtroom tension. This marks the end of Act 2 by completing the most dramatic testimony sequence before moving toward resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Sorkin employs a sophisticated dual-timeline structure that weaves between the 1969 courtroom proceedings and flashbacks to the 1968 Chicago protests. The script builds tension through rapid-fire cross-cutting between testimony and the events being described, creating a cinematic trial format where past and present inform each other. The structure serves both as legal procedural and historical recreation, with the courtroom serving as the primary arena where political ideologies clash. The pacing accelerates as the trial becomes increasingly contentious, culminating in the shocking treatment of Bobby Seale.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The opening prologue (scenes 1-36) establishes historical context through montage before settling into the trial format. The middle section uses rapid cross-cutting between courtroom testimony and Chicago flashbacks (scenes 44-122) to maintain momentum. The final act slows to focus on character conflicts and moral reckonings as the defendants face the consequences of their principles.

incitingIncident.description: The packed courtroom is introduced with defendants, lawyers, and security in place as Rennie Davis shows Tom Hayden his daily list of Americans killed since their arrest while Bobby Seale arrives in shackles.

validation.ruling: Scene #37 is just a brief transition moment with protesters and an egg-throwing incident. Scene #39 is the true inciting incident where the packed courtroom is introduced with all defendants, lawyers, and security in place - this is when the main conflict officially begins as the trial starts, shattering the defendants' ordinary world and forcing them into the central legal battle that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Kunstler systematically gets Daphne to admit that none of the defendants except Jerry and Rennie were present during the park incident, and that Jerry and Rennie were actually trying to turn people around and send them back down the hill.

validation.ruling: Scene #122 shows Kunstler's devastating cross-examination of undercover agent Daphne, systematically proving that none of the defendants were present at key violent incidents and that Jerry and Rennie were actually trying to calm the crowd. This represents a major shift from reactive defense to proactive offense, with the defense gaining significant ground and raising the stakes by exposing government deception.

climax.description: Abbie testifies about carrying ideas across state lines and quotes Lincoln about revolutionary rights, while Judge Hoffman repeatedly interrupts.

validation.ruling: Scene #190 (207 INT. COURTROOM - DAY) represents the climax as it occurs at 97% through the script and likely contains the final courtroom confrontation where the main story question is resolved. This is the ultimate dramatic peak where the trial reaches its conclusion and the defendants face their final judgment.

scenes.heading: 2 INT. LOTTERY DRAWING - DAY (FILE FOOTAGE) 2 2
scenes.summary: A young man from the Youth Draft Advisory Committee draws a capsule from a goldfish bowl and announces June 3rd as a draft lottery date while reporters discuss increased monthly draft totals.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. RURAL MAILBOX TREE - DAY 4 4
scenes.summary: A young black man sits slumped by rural mailboxes with his induction notice shaking in his hands while Martin Luther King's voice speaks about the war's impact on America's integrity.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. BALLROOM - NIGHT (FILE FOOTAGE) 5 5
scenes.summary: Martin Luther King continues his speech in a ballroom about Vietnam poisoning America's soul, ending with the rifle shot that killed him ringing out.

scenes.heading: 6 FAMOUS PHOTOGRAPH--(FILE FOOTAGE) 6 6
scenes.summary: A famous photograph shows three men pointing in unison toward where the shot came from.

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. CAMPAIGN RALLY - NIGHT (FILE FOOTAGE) 7 7
scenes.summary: Robert Kennedy speaks to a crowd in the rain about responding to King's death with love and wisdom, asking them to pray for King's family, before the gunshot that killed Kennedy is heard.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. AMBASSADOR HOTEL - NIGHT (FILE FOOTAGE) 8 8
scenes.summary: Screaming chaos engulfs the candidate at the Ambassador Hotel.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. LOTTERY DRAWING - DAY (FILE FOOTAGE) 9 9
scenes.summary: The young man at the lottery drawing announces April 22nd as another draft date.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. CAMPUS AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 10 10
scenes.summary: Rennie Davis speaks to a packed auditorium of conservatively dressed students, showing footage of Vietnamese villages and explaining that American napalm is destroying population centers with women and children.

scenes.heading: 11 UNDERGROUND CLUB - NIGHT 11 11
scenes.summary: Abbie Hoffman and Jerry Rubin discuss going to Chicago at a seedy underground club, with Jerry criticizing the war and Abbie predicting the police will lose their minds because Mayor Daley will wind them up.

scenes.heading: 12 EXT. SUBURBAN DRIVEWAY - DAY 12 12
scenes.summary: David Dellinger loads his station wagon while discussing non-violence with his wife and son, explaining he'll duck if police hit him.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. BLACK PANTHER HEADQUARTERS - NIGHT 13 13
scenes.summary: Bobby Seale prepares to leave Black Panther headquarters while telling his girlfriend Sondra that if authorities disrupt or intimidate them, the Panthers will not back down, despite her concerns about his planned Chicago speech.

scenes.heading: 14 CLOSE ON A TYPEWRITER 14 14
scenes.summary: An FBI confidential memo is typed discussing counter-intelligence action against New Left organizations and key activists.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. COLLEGE CLASSROOM - DAY 15 15
scenes.summary: Jerry Rubin demonstrates to students how to make a Molotov cocktail, explaining it's named after a Russian Commissar and starts with a glass bottle.

scenes.heading: 16 CLOSE ON TYPEWRITER 16 16
scenes.summary: The FBI memo continues being typed, discussing non-conformism in dress and speech and neglect of personal cleanliness.

scenes.heading: 17 COLLEGE CLASSROOM 17 17
scenes.summary: Jerry continues the Molotov cocktail demonstration, instructing students to pack styrofoam and add a cherry bomb.

scenes.heading: 18 CLOSE ON TYPEWRITER 18 18
scenes.summary: The FBI memo continues being typed, mentioning use of obscenities, drugs, and sexual promiscuity.

scenes.heading: 19 COLLEGE CLASSROOM 19 19
scenes.summary: Jerry instructs students to add chewing gum around the top of the bottle.

scenes.heading: 20 CLOSE ON TYPEWRITER 20 20
scenes.summary: The FBI memo concludes that these individuals are getting more brazen in their attempts to destroy American society.

scenes.heading: 21 COLLEGE CLASSROOM 21 21
scenes.summary: Jerry lights a cigarette and fixes it to the bottle with chewing gum as a fuse.

scenes.heading: 22 EXT. STREET - NIGHT 22 22
scenes.summary: Students throw Molotov cocktails at a U.S. Armed Forces Recruitment Center, with explosions lighting up the building facade.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. SDS OFFICE - NIGHT 23 23
scenes.summary: Tom Hayden studies a homemade map of the route from Grant Park to the convention center while telling Rennie Davis to inform Abbie they're going to Chicago to end the war and not mess around.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. CRASH PAD - SAME TIME 24 24
scenes.summary: Jerry Rubin relays Tom's message to Abbie Hoffman, who responds that he went to Brandeis and can do both.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. UNDERGROUND CLUB - NIGHT 25 25
scenes.summary: Abbie Hoffman addresses concerns about police overreaction at the underground club.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. PRESS ROOM - DAY (FILE FOOTAGE) 26 26
scenes.summary: Mayor Richard Daley announces his order to shoot to kill any arsonist or anyone with a Molotov cocktail.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. UNDERGROUND CLUB 27 27
scenes.summary: Abbie states they're not concerned about police overreaction.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. TARMAC - DAY (FILE FOOTAGE) 28 28
scenes.summary: A reporter announces that 5000 Illinois National Guard troops have been deployed to Chicago as rows of guardsmen come off transport planes.

scenes.heading: 29 INT. UNDERGROUND CLUB 29 29
scenes.summary: Abbie declares they're counting on police overreaction.

scenes.heading: 30 INT. PRESS CONFERENCE - DAY 30 30
scenes.summary: Tom Hayden announces at a press conference that they're coming to Chicago peacefully but will come whether they get permits or not.

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. TRAINING GROUND - DAY (FILE FOOTAGE) 31 31
scenes.summary: A reporter describes 10,000 additional Chicago police officers including riot squads as riot police practice with tear gas canisters.

scenes.heading: 32 INT. A DIFFERENT PRESS CONFERENCE - DAY 32 32
scenes.summary: Dave Dellinger announces at a press conference that they will storm the hearts and minds of the American people, not the convention with tanks or mace.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. CITY HALL PRESS CONFERENCE - DAY (FILE FOOTAGE) 33 33
scenes.summary: A government official declares the protesters are revolutionaries bent on destroying the United States of America.

scenes.heading: 34 INT. CONVENTION CENTER - NIGHT (FILE FOOTAGE) 34 34
scenes.summary: Walter Cronkite states that a Democratic Convention is about to begin in a police state as the title card reveals this is The Trial of the Chicago 7.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes the trial as the main narrative frame and sets the stakes

scenes.heading: 36 INT. MITCHELL’S OUTER OFFICE - DAY 36 36
scenes.summary: Federal prosecutors Richard Schultz and Thomas Foran wait in Attorney General John Mitchell's outer office while a workman replaces Lyndon Johnson's photo with Richard Nixon's.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. JOHN MITCHELL’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS 37 37
scenes.summary: Attorney General John Mitchell meets with prosecutors Foran and Schultz, discussing the Chicago riots and assigning them to prosecute the case despite Schultz's reservations about giving the defendants a stage.
scenes.turningPointNote: The prosecution is officially assigned, setting the legal machinery in motion

scenes.heading: 38 INT. MITCHELL’S OUTER-OFFICE - DAY 38 38
scenes.summary: Schultz and Foran exit Mitchell's office with Foran criticizing Schultz for not showing gratitude while Schultz worries about giving the defendants an audience.

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. COURTHOUSE - DAY 39 39
scenes.summary: A massive crowd outside the courthouse chants support and opposition to the defendants while holding various protest signs.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE ROTUNDA - SAME TIME A40
scenes.summary: Police officers escort Abbie Hoffman and Jerry Rubin through the courthouse rotunda as press and photographers are held back, with someone throwing an egg at Abbie that Jerry catches mid-air.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. COURTHOUSE CORRIDOR - DAY 40 40
scenes.summary: Defense attorneys William Kunstler and Leonard Weinglass meet with reporters in the courthouse corridor, with Kunstler introducing Weinglass as a First Amendment litigator.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. COURTROOM - CONTINUOUS 41 41
scenes.summary: The packed courtroom is introduced with defendants, lawyers, and security in place as Rennie Davis shows Tom Hayden his daily list of Americans killed since their arrest while Bobby Seale arrives in shackles.
scenes.turningPointNote: The trial officially begins with all parties present and Bobby Seale's dramatic entrance in shackles

scenes.heading: 42 INT. DEFENSE CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY 42 42
scenes.summary: In the defense conference room, Tom Hayden demands the group decide whether to use the trial to defend against serious charges or make a pointless statement, leading to heated debate about their strategy.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 43 43
scenes.summary: On trial day 3, prosecutor Schultz questions witness David Stahl about his occupation as the mayor's administrative officer and a March 26th meeting.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. STAHL’S OFFICE - DAY 44 44
scenes.summary: In flashback, Abbie Hoffman and Jerry Rubin meet with David Stahl in his office.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 45 45
scenes.summary: Stahl testifies that he was told about the Youth International Party's Festival of Life in Grant Park with rock bands during the Democratic Convention.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 46 46
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jerry Rubin tells Stahl that music will be performed, likely rock music.

scenes.heading: 47 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 47 47
scenes.summary: Stahl testifies that the defendants also mentioned there would be public fornication, prompting Schultz to ask him to repeat it.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. STAHL’S OFFICE - DAY 48 48
scenes.summary: In flashback, Jerry requests a parks permit for public fornication while Abbie adds rock music, with Stahl refusing and Abbie asking about R&B instead.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 49 49
scenes.summary: Stahl testifies that he did not issue the permits and is asked what Abbie Hoffman said when denied.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 50 50
scenes.summary: In flashback, Abbie tells Stahl the Festival of Life will happen regardless of permits, involving rock music, public fornication, and demands to stop sending friends to be slaughtered.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 51 51
scenes.summary: Schultz asks if Abbie Hoffman added something at the end of the meeting, with Stahl confirming he did.

scenes.heading: 52 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 52 52
scenes.summary: In flashback, Abbie offers to call off the whole event for a hundred thousand dollars.

scenes.heading: 53 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 53 53
scenes.summary: Prosecutor Schultz finishes questioning witness Stahl, and defense attorney Weinglass begins cross-examination asking about meetings with defendants.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 54 54
scenes.summary: Tom Hayden and Rennie Davis arrive at Stahl's office in coats and ties, introducing themselves.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 55 55
scenes.summary: Weinglass continues questioning Stahl about his meeting with David Dellinger on August 12th.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 56 56
scenes.summary: Stahl tells Dave Dellinger he'll give him the same response he gave the others.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 57 57
scenes.summary: Weinglass establishes that there were multiple meetings with defendants requesting permits, all recorded in City Hall logs, and all requests were denied.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 58 58
scenes.summary: Dave Dellinger tells Stahl they intend a peaceful demonstration and aren't interested in violence or disturbing delegates.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 59 59
scenes.summary: Weinglass confirms that at each meeting, the request for permits was denied.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. STAHL'S OFFICE - DAY 60 60
scenes.summary: Stahl refuses Tom and Rennie's request to demonstrate near the Hilton, and Tom warns that demonstrations will happen anyway, leading Stahl to ask if that's a threat.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 61 61
scenes.summary: Weinglass establishes Stahl takes orders from Mayor Daley and serves at his pleasure, then Judge Hoffman forces Bobby Seale to identify Fred Hampton as head of the Chicago Black Panthers.
scenes.turningPointNote: Judge Hoffman's targeting of Bobby Seale and forcing identification of Black Panther leadership escalates the political persecution angle

scenes.heading: INT. MAKE-SHIFT PRESS ROOM - EARLY EVENING A62
scenes.summary: Abbie and Jerry hold a press conference while Kunstler arrives at the conspiracy office amid waiting photographers.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - SAME TIME 63 63
scenes.summary: The conspiracy office buzzes with activity as young women work phones and handle mail, while Kunstler warns against calling it the conspiracy office and Bernadine defends the name choice.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS 64 64
scenes.summary: Kunstler tells Tom and Weinglass not to hold press conferences, and the lawyers identify jurors 6 and 11 as potentially sympathetic based on a James Baldwin book and nodding during cross-examination.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - CONTINUOUS 65 65
scenes.summary: The press conference plays on TV where Abbie drops his comedic tone and says his price to call off the revolution is his life.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 66 66
scenes.summary: Everyone waits in the courtroom as Judge Hoffman receives a note from bailiff and marshal, then adjourns court to see counsel in chambers.

scenes.heading: 67 INT. JUDGE HOFFMAN’S CHAMBERS - DAY 67 67
scenes.summary: Judge Hoffman reveals that jurors 6 and 11 received threatening notes allegedly from Black Panthers, then dismisses both jurors despite their willingness to continue serving.
scenes.turningPointNote: The dismissal of the two sympathetic jurors fundamentally changes the trial's dynamics and suggests possible prosecutorial misconduct

scenes.heading: 68 INT. JUDGE HOFFMAN’S OUTER-OFFICE - DAY 68 68
scenes.summary: Kunstler accuses Foran's office of sending the threatening letters, warning he'll expose them if he finds proof, while Schultz and Foran exchange uncertain looks.

scenes.heading: 69 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 69 69
scenes.summary: The defense team reviews alternate jurors and discovers Kay Richards is dating someone who works for Mayor Daley, causing all defendants to erupt in outrage.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 70 70
scenes.summary: Abbie and Jerry appear in court wearing judge's robes as an homage, then reveal police uniforms underneath when ordered to remove the robes, drawing laughter from the gallery.

scenes.heading: 71 EXT. HILTON HOTEL - NIGHT (TOM’S MEMORY) 71 71
scenes.summary: Tom remembers riot police removing their name tags and badges before confronting demonstrators at the Hilton Hotel.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 72 72
scenes.summary: Detective DeLuca testifies that he was assigned to surveil Rennie Davis and observed Tom Hayden committing a crime.

scenes.heading: 73 INT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 73 73
scenes.summary: Grant Park at night fills with thousands of protesters setting up tents, burning draft cards and bras, while police announce the 11pm park closure over loudspeakers.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 74 74
scenes.summary: DeLuca continues testifying about spotting Rennie Davis in the crowd and returning to find something at their car.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 75 75
scenes.summary: Tom is caught letting air out of the detectives' tire with his key, and DeLuca slams him against the car hood while ordering him to spread his legs.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 76 76
scenes.summary: Schultz asks DeLuca what happened next during Tom's arrest.

scenes.heading: 77 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 77 77
scenes.summary: A demonstrator shouts that police are hassling Tom Hayden, causing two dozen people to move toward the arrest scene while DeLuca orders Tom to tell them to get back.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 78 78
scenes.summary: Schultz asks DeLuca whether Tom told the crowd to get back.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 79 79
scenes.summary: Tom shouts for everyone to get back and stay cool, saying he's alright.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 80 80
scenes.summary: DeLuca testifies that Tom was egging the crowd on and explains why he didn't arrest Tom immediately.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 81 81
scenes.summary: A TV camera light suddenly shines on DeLuca during the arrest, and Bell identifies it as a television camera.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 82 82
scenes.summary: DeLuca testifies they decided to arrest Tom the next morning to diffuse the situation.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. GRANT STATUE - DAY 83 83
scenes.summary: Tom sits with Rennie and friends when police cars arrive with flashing lights, and Tom jokes about telling the officers about SaraBeth's parents before being arrested.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 84 84
scenes.summary: Bobby Seale attempts to make a pro se motion to defend himself, citing legal precedent, while a young Black woman and Fred Hampton help pass him notes.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 85 85
scenes.summary: Abbie performs stand-up comedy about Tom's arrest, joking that protesters mobilized to free Tom but couldn't find their way out of the park.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 86 86
scenes.summary: Prosecutor Schultz begins questioning witness Stanley Wojohowski.

scenes.heading: 87 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 87 87
scenes.summary: A biker named Eddie introduces Stan Wojohowski to Abbie as a bodyguard who can handle himself.

scenes.heading: 88 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 88 88
scenes.summary: Wojohowski reveals he is actually a Chicago Police Officer.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 89 89
scenes.summary: A man introduces someone named Sam to Rennie, saying Sam can be trusted.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 90 90
scenes.summary: Sam reveals he is Detective Sam McGiven of the Chicago Police Department.

scenes.heading: 91 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 91 91
scenes.summary: Scotty Scibelli introduces himself to Tom, offering to provide drugs and other services.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 92 92
scenes.summary: Scott reveals he is Staff Sergeant Scott Scibelli of the Illinois State Police.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that multiple trusted allies were actually undercover law enforcement officers exposes the extent of government infiltration

scenes.heading: 93 INT. BAR - NIGHT 93 93
scenes.summary: Jerry meets a woman named Daphne at a bar who buys him a Tom Collins, and they begin flirting over the drink.

scenes.heading: 94 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 94 94
scenes.summary: Daphne reveals she is Special Agent Daphne O'Connor of FBI Counter Intelligence, testifying that her assignment was to use Jerry to infiltrate the protest leaders.

scenes.heading: 95 EXT. MICHIGAN AVENUE - DAY 95 95
scenes.summary: Daphne testifies that she was with the four defendants leading about eight hundred protesters to Police Headquarters on August 27th.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. POLICE STATION - DAY 96 96
scenes.summary: Abbie, Jerry, Rennie, Daphne and other undercovers lead demonstrators chanting 'Free Tom Hayden' as they pour onto Michigan Avenue, while a policeman calls ahead on his radio.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 97 97
scenes.summary: Police officers grab riot gear including helmets and nightsticks from racks in preparation.

scenes.heading: 98 EXT. MICHIGAN AVENUE - DAY 98 98
scenes.summary: Detective Sam testifies that Allen Ginsburg was at the front of the group chanting what he calls a war chant.

scenes.heading: 99 OMIT 99 99
scenes.summary: Allen Ginsburg chants 'ohmmmmm' with raised hands while Abbie explains to Jerry that he's calming the energy and settling things down.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 100 100
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted from the script.

scenes.heading: 101 EXT. MICHIGAN AVENUE - DAY 101 101
scenes.summary: Abbie performs comedy about the testimony claiming Ginsburg's chanting was a war signal to beat poets to pelt troopers with blank verse.

scenes.heading: 102 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 102 102
scenes.summary: A young woman waves an American flag while being carried on a demonstrator's shoulders as frat boys on the sidewalk scream at her to put the flag down and make sandwiches, while Jerry wants to confront them but Abbie says they're not the enemy.

scenes.heading: 103 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 103 103
scenes.summary: Abbie tells an audience they make a right on 11th Street.

scenes.heading: 104 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 104 104
scenes.summary: Daphne testifies that's when they saw it.

scenes.heading: 105 EXT. 11TH STREET - DAY 105 105
scenes.summary: Abbie, Jerry, Dave and Rennie encounter riot police forming a line in the street, with Jerry wanting to rush them while others argue against it and try to turn the crowd around.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 106 106
scenes.summary: Abbie explains to the audience that marshals are spreading word to keep moving left on Roosevelt back to the park.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 107 107
scenes.summary: Daphne testifies that when they got to the park they saw three divisions of police officers had moved in from the south.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 108 108
scenes.summary: Jerry, Rennie and the demonstrators approach and see lines of police officers formed at the top of the hill in the park.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 109 109
scenes.summary: Abbie tells the audience that trouble happens when you don't give protesters a place to go.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 110 110
scenes.summary: Schultz questions a witness about the crowd's mood while Kunstler objects and Abbie and Jerry lead the gallery in shouting 'Overruled' before Judge Hoffman threatens to clear the courtroom.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 111 111
scenes.summary: Jerry shouts insults at police calling them pigs while Rennie suggests leaving their children out of it.

scenes.heading: 112 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 112 112
scenes.summary: Scott testifies about hearing racial slurs shouted at the police.

scenes.heading: 113 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 113 113
scenes.summary: Jerry continues shouting provocatively at armed police while Rennie expresses concern about the guns and refuses to back Jerry's challenge to fight.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 114 114
scenes.summary: Abbie tells the audience that the fraternity boys who were hassling the girl are back.

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 115 115
scenes.summary: Frat boys shout misogynistic insults about the flag while Rennie asks Jerry to calm the crowd and Daphne urges Jerry to defuse the situation.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 116 116
scenes.summary: Daphne testifies that someone from the crowd shouts something.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 117 117
scenes.summary: Abbie tells the audience that a guy somewhere in the crowd shouts something.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 118 118
scenes.summary: Scott testifies it may have been Jerry Rubin who shouted something, prompting objections from both defense lawyers before the judge sustains the objection.

scenes.heading: 119 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 119 119
scenes.summary: Someone in the crowd shouts 'Take the hill!' causing a land rush as the crowd charges up the hill toward riot police while Jerry and Rennie run after them trying to stop them.

scenes.heading: 120 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 120 120
scenes.summary: Abbie describes tear gas and riot clubs to the audience, explaining their painful effects.

scenes.heading: 121 EXT. GRANT PARK - DAY 121 121
scenes.summary: Police fire tear gas and attack the crowd with clubs while Jerry and Rennie try to pull people away, and Jerry flies into action when he sees three frat boys tackle and assault the young woman with the flag.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 122 122
scenes.summary: Kunstler begins cross-examining Daphne in silence before asking if Tom, Abbie and Dave returned to the park after bailing Tom out.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. PARK - EARLY EVENING 123 123
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, Jerry, Rennie and Dave survey the aftermath of the battle with Tom expressing worry about getting everyone out of Chicago alive while Abbie dismisses responsibility and Jerry explains he wasn't arrested but detained.

scenes.heading: 124 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 124 124
scenes.summary: Kunstler systematically gets Daphne to admit that none of the defendants except Jerry and Rennie were present during the park incident, and that Jerry and Rennie were actually trying to turn people around and send them back down the hill.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key testimony undermines prosecution case by establishing most defendants weren't present and those who were tried to prevent violence

scenes.heading: 126 EXT. PARK BENCH - DAY 126 126
scenes.summary: Abbie and Jerry encounter prosecutor Schultz with his young daughters in the park during Christmas time, leading to a friendly conversation where Abbie compliments Schultz and discusses the trial.

scenes.heading: EXT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT A151
scenes.summary: All the lights are off at the conspiracy office and a phone is ringing.

scenes.heading: INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT B151
scenes.summary: Bernadine answers the ringing phone while Tom wakes up from sleeping on the couch, and she tells Tom that Bill is calling with news that something has happened.

scenes.heading: 151 INT. COOK COUNTY JAIL - VISITING ROOM - MORNING 151 151
scenes.summary: Kunstler informs Bobby Seale that Fred Hampton was shot and killed in a police raid, leading to a conversation where Bobby explains how the other defendants' rebellion against their fathers differs from his own situation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Fred Hampton's death escalates the stakes and Bobby articulates the racial divide in their shared struggle

scenes.heading: 152 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 152 152
scenes.summary: Detective Frapoly testifies about Bobby Seale's speech while Bobby repeatedly objects to testimony against him due to being denied counsel, leading to Bobby being removed from the courtroom.

scenes.heading: 153 INT. HOLDING CELL - DAY 153 153
scenes.summary: Bobby is thrown into a holding cell and the door slams behind him.

scenes.heading: 154 INT. COURTROOM - SAME TIME 154 154
scenes.summary: Tense silence fills the courtroom as everyone waits.

scenes.heading: 155 INT. HOLDING CELL - SAME TIME 155 155
scenes.summary: Quick shots show Bobby being put in restraints on his wrists and ankles.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM - SAME TIME A156
scenes.summary: The defendants pass notes agreeing not to stand for Judge Hoffman while Abbie and Jerry stare angrily at the judge.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING CELL - SAME TIME B156
scenes.summary: A rag is stuffed in Bobby's mouth and cloth is tied around his face.

scenes.heading: 156 INT. COURTROOM - SAME TIME 156 156
scenes.summary: Marshals bring Bobby into the courtroom bound, gagged and chained, horrifying the gallery, while Judge Hoffman asks for assurance Bobby won't disrupt the trial and Bobby shakes his head no.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bobby Seale's binding and gagging represents the complete breakdown of legal process and constitutional rights

scenes.heading: 158 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - SAME TIME 158 158
scenes.summary: At the conspiracy office, the defendants argue about Tom standing for the judge, with Jerry questioning Tom's actions while Tom defends respecting institutions and Abbie declares it's a revolution.

scenes.heading: 160 INT. FOYER - CONTINUOUS 160 160
scenes.summary: Jane greets the lawyers and Tom at the door, offering coffee and directing them to the study while privately thanking Tom for standing for the judge.

scenes.heading: 161 INT. STUDY - DAY 161 161
scenes.summary: Ramsey Clark meets with Kunstler, Weinglass and Tom in his study alongside Justice Department deputies, explaining he invited the government representatives to avoid any appearance of impropriety.

scenes.heading: 162 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 162 162
scenes.summary: Kunstler calls Ramsey Clark as a witness but Schultz moves to disallow him, leading to a legal argument about federal regulations while Justice Department representatives watch from the gallery.

scenes.heading: 163 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 163 163
scenes.summary: The dejected defendants discuss strategy with Jerry suggesting they get arrested in solidarity with Dave, while Tom reveals Kunstler wants him to testify, leading to tension about who should take the stand.

scenes.heading: 164 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 164 164
scenes.summary: Dave introduces a speaker to a large crowd while Tom notices a young person climbing a flagpole in the back.

scenes.heading: 165 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 165 165
scenes.summary: Kunstler questions Tom about attracting underage minors and his military service during a mock cross-examination at the conspiracy office.

scenes.heading: 166 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 166 166
scenes.summary: Dave continues his speech while police move in to stop the kid climbing the flagpole and Rennie tries to intervene, shouting that it's just a kid.

scenes.heading: 167 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 167 167
scenes.summary: Tom testifies that the police were shoving Rennie away.

scenes.heading: 168 EXT. GRANT PARK/FLAG POLE - NIGHT 168 168
scenes.summary: An officer tells Rennie the situation doesn't concern him while Rennie asks them to leave the kid alone and another officer recognizes Rennie.

scenes.heading: INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT A169
scenes.summary: Tom explains to Kunstler that Rennie was trying to get the police off the kid.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT B169
scenes.summary: Tom watches helplessly as officers pin the kid against the flagpole and an officer punches the kid in the groin twice, causing Rennie to grab and pull the officer off the kid.

scenes.heading: 169 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 169 169
scenes.summary: Tom begins to describe something that came out of nowhere.

scenes.heading: INT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT A170
scenes.summary: A nightstick cracks Rennie across the face, sending blood flying.

scenes.heading: INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT B170
scenes.summary: Tom sarcastically comments on six armed police officers versus Rennie Davis and a pocket protector while Kunstler prepares to play a tape recording.

scenes.heading: 170 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 170 170
scenes.summary: Froines steps to the microphone asking police to respect their permit and calling for medics while the camera pushes in on Tom reaching his breaking point.

scenes.heading: 171 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 171 171
scenes.summary: Kunstler plays a tape recording and notes that Dellinger tried to stop Tom from saying what he was about to say to the crowd.

scenes.heading: 172 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 172 172
scenes.summary: Dave tells Tom that Rennie has been beaten and urges him to tell the crowd to stay calm, but Tom refuses.

scenes.heading: 173 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 173 173
scenes.summary: Kunstler asks Tom if he told the crowd to stay calm.

scenes.heading: INT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT A174
scenes.summary: Tom takes the microphone and announces to the crowd that Rennie Davis has been beaten by police and his skull cracked open.

scenes.heading: INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT B174
scenes.summary: Kunstler presses Tom about whether he told the crowd to stay calm, and Tom begins to respond about blood flowing.

scenes.heading: 174 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 174 174
scenes.summary: Tom shouts into the microphone that if blood is going to flow, let it flow all over the city, and tells the crowd to go to the Convention and get on the street.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tom's inflammatory speech escalates the situation from peaceful protest to riot

scenes.heading: 175 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 175 175
scenes.summary: Kunstler questions whether Tom's statement about blood flowing was an order to start a peaceful demonstration.

scenes.heading: 176 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 176 176
scenes.summary: A line of riot police climbs a hill and sees an army of protestors coming toward them.

scenes.heading: 177 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 177 177
scenes.summary: Kunstler asks if Tom tried to stop people once he had settled down.

scenes.heading: 178 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 178 178
scenes.summary: The army of protestors approaches the riot police, who fire tear gas and beat protestors with batons.

scenes.heading: 179 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 179 179
scenes.summary: Tom admits to Kunstler that he didn't try to stop anyone.

scenes.heading: 180 EXT. GRANT PARK - NIGHT 180 180
scenes.summary: Tom moves through the crowd directing protestors to head to the bridges while bloody faces lie on the ground.

scenes.heading: 181 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 181 181
scenes.summary: Kunstler confirms Tom sent people to the footbridges and asks if they knew what was waiting for them.

scenes.heading: 182 EXT. FOOTBRIDGE - NIGHT 182 182
scenes.summary: Protestors reach one end of a footbridge and see jeeps outfitted with concertina wire approaching from the other direction.

scenes.heading: 183 EXT. ANOTHER FOOTBRIDGE - SAME TIME 183 183
scenes.summary: Another group of protestors meets a line of National Guardsmen with bayonets.

scenes.heading: 184 EXT. THIRD FOOTBRIDGE - SAME TIME 184 184
scenes.summary: A third group of protestors encounters armored vehicles covered with National Guardsmen.

scenes.heading: 185 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 185 185
scenes.summary: Kunstler and Tom discuss how all access to the convention was blocked by the National Guard and when Tom stopped being one of the good guys.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIRST FOOTBRIDGE - NIGHT A186
scenes.summary: Glass bottles fly through the air at police while Dave moves through the crowd shouting for people not to throw anything.

scenes.heading: INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT (FILE FOOTAGE) B186
scenes.summary: Tom tells Kunstler that some people threw bottles but Dave tried to stop it, and that he and others found an unguarded bridge.

scenes.heading: 186 EXT. SMALL FOOTBRIDGE - NIGHT 186 186
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, Jerry and others throw over a police barricade and cross a dark, empty bridge.

scenes.heading: 187 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 187 187
scenes.summary: Abbie tells an audience that this is where things got weird.

scenes.heading: 188 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - DAY 188 188
scenes.summary: Kunstler states that Tom, Abbie, Jerry and others found the only way to the convention.

scenes.heading: 189 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 189 189
scenes.summary: Abbie describes the Haymarket Tavern as a watering hole for Chicago's political class packed to watch Humphrey's nomination.

scenes.heading: 190 INT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 190 190
scenes.summary: The Haymarket Tavern is crowded with lawyers and dates watching the roll call vote on television and toasting.

scenes.heading: 191 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 191 191
scenes.summary: Abbie explains that one side of the tavern has a huge picture window with smoked glass that prevents seeing inside from the street.

scenes.heading: 192 EXT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 192 192
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, Jerry and others cross an intersection clogged with police barricades and vehicles to reach a darker area.

scenes.heading: 193 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 193 193
scenes.summary: Kunstler notes they made it through riot police, tear gas, and national guard to reach sight of the Convention Center.

scenes.heading: 194 EXT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 194 194
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, Jerry and others find themselves surrounded by riot police appearing from both directions on a dark sidewalk.

scenes.heading: 195 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 195 195
scenes.summary: Tom tells Kunstler they were trapped, and Kunstler suggests another word for trapped is 'caught.'

scenes.heading: 196 EXT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 196 196
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, Jerry and others are pinned against the picture window by approaching riot police.

scenes.heading: 197 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 197 197
scenes.summary: Abbie describes the contrast between the 1950s atmosphere inside the bar and the 1960s being performed outside.

scenes.heading: 198 INT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 198 198
scenes.summary: A woman in the bar notices the protestors outside while patrons discuss buckeyes, and more people start noticing the situation.

scenes.heading: 199 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 199 199
scenes.summary: Abbie says they see a cop do something you never want to see a cop do.

scenes.heading: 200 EXT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 200 200
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, and Jerry watch as riot officers remove their badges and name tags and put them in their pockets.

scenes.heading: 201 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 201 201
scenes.summary: Kunstler questions why only Tom, Abbie and Jerry saw the officers remove their badges, and Tom says it was dark and people were scared.

scenes.heading: 202 INT. COLLEGE AUDITORIUM - NIGHT 202 202
scenes.summary: Abbie describes the contrast as an unnecessary metaphor between the decades inside and outside the bar.

scenes.heading: 203 INT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 203 203
scenes.summary: Tom, Abbie, Jerry and the demonstrators are pushed through the window which smashes as riot police follow them inside while patrons scream.
scenes.turningPointNote: The violent climax of the riot as protestors are literally pushed through a window into the bar

scenes.heading: 204 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 204 204
scenes.summary: Kunstler and Tom argue simultaneously about whether Tom was resisting arrest and the violence that occurred.

scenes.heading: 205 INT. HAYMARKET TAVERN - NIGHT 205 205
scenes.summary: Tom sits bleeding in the rubble and sees Abbie handcuffed against a wall as a policeman orders Tom's hands behind his back.

scenes.heading: 206 INT. CONSPIRACY OFFICE - NIGHT 206 206
scenes.summary: Tom reveals he meant to say 'our blood' instead of just 'blood,' and Abbie realizes Tom was saying if they were going to be beaten, everyone should see it.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that Tom's inflammatory speech was actually about their own victimization, not inciting violence

scenes.heading: 207 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 207 207
scenes.summary: Abbie testifies about carrying ideas across state lines and quotes Lincoln about revolutionary rights, while Judge Hoffman repeatedly interrupts.

scenes.heading: 208 INT. COURTROOM - DAY 208 208
scenes.summary: The defendants enter in prison coveralls for sentencing, and Judge Hoffman allows Tom to speak for the group, praising his respect and remorse.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final sentencing scene where Judge Hoffman unexpectedly praises Tom

structuralWarnings.text: Act 3 is only 11% of the script — structural placement suspect
2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
